mechanism_narrative: With the federal funds rate pinned near zero (0.18% as of June 2010), the near-total absence of a discount-rate floor compresses equity risk premia and supports a persistent upward drift in equity prices over a quarterly horizon, as investors reach for yield in risk assets while cash returns near zero.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.18% in June 2010, near zero lower bound
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during any 12-month period in which FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, or rank IC falls below 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate remains elevated above 9% while nonfarm payrolls fail to show sustained monthly gains, weak labor income growth signals fragile consumer demand ahead, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when the trigger condition is active, or rank IC is not statistically distinguishable from zero over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in retail sales growth and housing starts indicates a joint contraction in consumer and residential investment demand that is not visible from either series alone; when both RSAFS momentum and HOUST levels deteriorate together, forward equity returns turn negative over the next month as aggregate demand deceleration broadens.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 583k (revised) May 2010, below pre-crisis norms
regime_hint: demand-side fragility during fiscal stimulus withdrawal
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes produce positive or flat 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power over either single series alone (delta rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: During an early-cycle recovery following a deep recession, sustained month-over-month increases in industrial production signal reviving manufacturing activity and corporate earnings capacity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon as capacity utilization and profit expectations rise together.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO recovering to 91.99 (revised) by June 2010 from 2009 trough
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns fail to outperform the unconditional base rate (65.5% positive) during periods of sustained positive INDPRO momentum, or rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI inflation decelerates toward levels consistent with disinflation or deflation risk, markets price rising uncertainty about demand destruction and policy effectiveness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month until inflation stabilizes.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL revised value 217.29, low year-over-year growth mid-2010
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of low-CPI-growth episodes, or the effect disappears when tested outside the 2009-2011 disinflation window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month trend in total nonfarm payrolls reflects improving labor market momentum that feeds directly into consumer spending expectations, producing a short-term positive drift in S&P 500 prices over the following trading week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns show no significant positive association with rising 3-month PAYEMS trend (rank IC < 0.02) across a 5-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts below prior cyclical lows signals renewed contraction in residential construction and household wealth effects, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon as the housing sector historically leads broader economic downturns.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 583k May 2010, near post-crisis lows
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns after HOUST breaches the 550k threshold are positive in more than 55% of instances, invalidating the leading-indicator claim.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned near zero while the unemployment rate remains persistently above 9%, markets anticipate further unconventional monetary easing (quantitative easing) to support growth, and this joint liquidity-trap condition produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as investors front-run expected policy accommodation.
regime_hint: liquidity trap with anticipated unconventional easing
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns are not significantly positive (p > 0.05) during joint-trigger episodes, or the interaction effect vanishes relative to either variable in isolation (delta rank IC < 0.02).
